title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
summary: Kagenti addresses security risks in multi-agent AI systems by implementing identity-based security with delegation chains and open source infrastructure. The approach prevents data leaks and invisible breaches in production agentic AI deployments.
writeup: This IBM Technology video explains Kagenti, an open source approach to securing multi-agent AI systems through identity-based security and delegation chains. It addresses how production agentic deployments can suffer data leaks and invisible breaches when agents act on behalf of users and other agents without verifiable identity and trust boundaries. The key takeaways: assigning identity to agents, tracking delegation chains across handoffs, and applying zero-trust principles to limit what each agent can access and do.
